Key Takeaways:
• Creating scarcity in Evergreen Launches can be tricky. As you know this (scarcity)
can be one of the most powerful mental triggers.
• Generally Evergreen Launches are awesome, but you do give up certain aspects of a
normal launch and it’s usually because of a lack of scarcity.
• You should always include some form of scarcity - even for an Evergreen Launch.
• Generally, there are 3 ways to create scarcity:
1. Close the cart
2. Increase the price
3. Remove the bonuses
• You can use any combination, but it’s highly recommended that you use a model
that works for your market. There can be holes in this model and that is OK - some
people will find a way to opt back in and get the special pricing at a later time.
• You can only do so much and will have to just put together the best strategy you
can.
• You can also rotate offers:
o For example, on week 1 you could offer a bonus report
o On week 2 they could get a bonus call
o On week 3 they could get a bonus DVD
• Depending on which week they come into your world they would get a different
offer. If certain people don’t buy you can give them a break for a week and then try
them again with a different offer.
• There’s a lot of flexibility here, and there’s no set rules. See what works best in your
market.
• But, the most important point is that you absolutely want to make sure you include
some type of scarcity in your Evergreen Launch.

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now I want to talk about scarcity with Evergreen Launches, because this is one of
the trickiest things is how to deal with scarcity. From going through Product Launch
Formula, as you know, scarcity is one of the most powerful mental triggers there is,
especially when it comes down to the launch, the opening cart and especially the closing
cart. Lots of times these days we’re seeing the final day of the cart open being bigger than
the first day because of the scarcity, so scarcity is really powerful.
So, how do we do that if there’s an Evergreen Launch? Generally, the rule of
thumb is Evergreen Launches are awesome, there’s lots of cool things about them, but
you do give up a little bit and one of them is you give up a little bit in terms of scarcity in
many markets.
So I think the best way to deal with this is – let’s say this is your Evergreen
Launch and first we have the squeeze page, and then they come in here and here’s
Prelaunch Content one, then two, then three, and we go through here. So if this person
joining your list is fred@gmail or wherever, if they join your list here then they run
through your sequence and that sequence ends up on this sales letter, you open the cart,
they go to the sales letter, and then they’ve got (whatever) three days until this is the cart
close where the cart closes.
So basically Fred goes through this prelaunch and now you give him this offer
and he has three days to take advantage of whatever that special offer is. As we know
from Product Launch Formula there is basically three primary ways to develop scarcity.
One is the product goes off the market, the other one is the price increases, and the third
one is bonuses go away.
Of course, you can do all of those or a couple of those, whatever you want. But,
those are the main ways of developing scarcity for your offer.
So this is the cart close right here and you have scarcity. Fred joins your launch
list and Fred basically has got these three days, and let’s just say that the price is going
up. Let’s just say if he takes advantage during this period of time then he gets the thing
for (whatever) $97 instead $147. That’s the launch special. Maybe there’s an extra bonus
too, maybe it’s a bonus call or something like that.
If he takes advantage of this he joins this, let’s just say it’s June 1st he joins, the
launch goes and the cart opens on June 8th and the cart closes on June 11th. I’m just
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
making all this stuff up. That means he’s got from June 8th to June 11th to go ahead and
take advantage of this and get the special pricing and get the special bonus.
This is no different from how the real world acts. A lot of times if you sign up for a
magazine subscription you get special pricing or you can renew at special pricing. It
doesn’t matter when you sign up, it’s just that offer is available when you come into the
universe of that, when you become a prospect. This is the same way.
Now, let’s just say a week later Jane joins your list. So, she’s a week later, so that
means that she’s getting this offer on June 15th, the cart opens and we said it was going
to be open for four days, so that means it closes on June 19th. That means Jane gets this
deal, the $97 and the bonus call, between June 15th and June 19th.
Fred comes back and he’s either going to get “it’s not available, the cart is closed,”
or he’s going to get this pricing ($147), because you set up special pages just for the next
launch, just for the next time – sort of like in the last video where I walk through there’s
a launch each week. Here Fred is getting the launch one week, Jane is getting the launch
the next week. Fred comes back, you’re not going to give him the launch pricing or the
launch bonuses.
So that’s, in my opinion, probably the cleanest way to handle scarcity. It’s based
on when they came into your universe.
Now, if Fred is really smart and he figures out what’s going on, he might go and
use a different email address and come through here the next week and he’s able to get
your launch special the next week as well. It’s not a perfect world, those types of things
can happen and depending on your market they probably will.
If you’re selling stuff to the knitting market they’re probably not going to figure
this stuff out, but if you’re selling stuff into the internet marketing world a few of them
are going to figure that out probably or they might just come across your site six months
later. This is totally going to happen to you no matter what market you’re in. They’re
going to come across your site six months later and they’re going to join your list and
they’re going to go through that sequence again.
If that happens the reality is that they’re probably not even going to remember
that they went through it the first time, people are just not as wrapped up in your
material as you are. The reality is that a lot of people that join your list they’re not even
going to follow through and get your Prelaunch Content. Sometimes people in June they
might not be ready to see your content, they join your list again in December and maybe
they’re ready to see your content at that time.

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
That’s just the reality. Not everyone who joins your list is going to go through
your entire sequence. Certainly if someone goes through your sequence a few months
apart they’re not going to remember the material, trust me. I’ve had a lot of experience in
this and for the most part 1% might actually remember having seen your stuff. Even if
they remember having seen your stuff, they’re not going to remember the pricing
difference. So that’s probably the best way to handle scarcity.
Another thing you can do if you’re doing say the weekly launches, or especially
the monthly launch, you could limit the scarcity based on a number, on class size or
quantity available. You have to be a little more hands on, but you can absolutely do that.
For a couple years every time I rolled out Product Launch Formula I only allowed 500
people to come, because based on our customer service and our interaction that was
about the number that worked out well for us, so we cut it off at 500 each time.
That was doing a launch every six months, every eight months, or every nine
months. You could do the same thing if you’re launching every single month, you could
just say, “This is the quantity we have.” John Gallagher, the food stamps to six figures
guy, he did a monthly launch for years, I think he’s probably still doing it for his herbal
kits. These are physical kits and he can only make up so many in a month. And then each
month he’ll basically do a launch, the new people into his list will get that sequence and it
will sell the kits, but there’s only a limited number.
You can also limit number based on class size or quantity available or bonuses
available. You could also do it limited based on class start. So maybe you’re starting a
new class every week or maybe you’re starting a new class every month, that means they
have to join wherever, between June 8th and June 11th, or they have to wait until the
next class starts. That could work well if you’re doing it every month or every quarter.
Another way you could do it is you can rotate offers. So, if we go back to in that
last video where I was talking about basically where you do a launch every week, I walk
through that, here’s week one and week two, so this is launch number one, this is week
two, this is launch number two, and on and on.
Maybe this week for this sequence you’ve got some special bonus report, if they
buy they get this special report or this special DVD. Maybe this week they get a bonus
call. Maybe this week they get a bonus DVD. So when they come into your world here,
you make this offer. When they come into the world they get this offer. When they come
into the world there they get a different offer.
Or you could have pricing or whatever. So, you can basically switch up your offers
each week depending on when people come into your world and then you can run people

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
through this sequence, and then you can maybe give them a break and the people that
joined for that launch if they didn’t buy you can try them for this sequence, because
maybe they’ll like that bonus. So there’s a lot of mixing and matching that can be done
throughout this whole thing with the scarcity.
The number one thing with the scarcity, the way I approach it is they get the deal
based on when they come into your universe. If they’re smart enough or they figure it out
that they can enter your universe again with a different email address, then they go
through your sequence again. So, there you go, scarcity for Evergreen Launches.
